title: Stdlib math builtins polish (clamp/lerp/remap)
analysis:
Task 356 is a code-review follow-up that polishes the clamp/lerp/remap builtins in reify-stdlib. The worktree branch has already removed all clamp/lerp/remap/mod implementations and their tests (~530 lines) from main, providing a clean slate for re-implementation with improvements.

Key issues to fix:
1. **remap dimension stripping**: remap uses as_f64() on all args, silently dropping Scalar dimension. Need dimension-aware Scalar arms mirroring clamp/lerp.
2. **NaN guard completeness**: clamp guards NaN in lo/hi but not x; lerp passes NaN t through to sanitize_value. Need explicit x.is_nan() in clamp and NaN check on t after extraction in lerp.
3. **Helper extraction**: (a) quinary_f64 for remap, (b) valid_f64_range(lo,hi) for repeated NaN+inverted guard, (c) lerp_f64(a,b,t) for repeated formula, (d) assert_real_approx!/assert_scalar_approx! test macros.
4. **Additional coverage**: clamp Int below-lo/above-hi, lerp NaN in a/b, lerp Int inputs, remap Scalar inputs.
5. **Minor cleanup**: Move mod to two-arg section, add lerp Int fast path, remap early-exit for from_lo==from_hi, Scalar reconstruction in clamp fallback.

The existing codebase convention is: (a) helper functions (unary, binary, etc.) handle arg count, (b) builtins pattern-match on Value variants with explicit Scalar arms for dimension, (c) NaN/inf always becomes Undef via guards or sanitize_value, (d) fallback uses as_f64() after checking dimension consistency. Memory confirms stdlib min/max previously dropped Scalar dimension via as_f64() fallback and was fixed by adding explicit Scalar match arms.

prerequisites.description: Add assert_real_approx! and assert_scalar_approx! test helper macros in the #[cfg(test)] module to reduce the ~20 repeated match-on-Real/Scalar assertion blocks to one-liners
Restore the ternary(args, f) helper function (was removed along with clamp/lerp in the branch diff). Place after binary() definition
Add valid_f64_range(lo: f64, hi: f64) -> bool helper: returns false if either is NaN or lo > hi. Used by clamp Real/Scalar/fallback arms
Add lerp_f64(a: f64, b: f64, t: f64) -> f64 helper: computes a + t * (b - a). Used by lerp Real/Scalar/fallback arms
Add quinary_f64(args, f) helper: extracts 5 f64 args via as_f64(), returns Undef on wrong count or extraction failure, applies sanitize_value to result. Used by remap

steps.description: Write failing tests for mod: mod_basic, mod_exact_division, mod_negative_dividend, mod_negative_divisor, mod_by_zero_returns_undef, mod_non_int_returns_undef, mod_wrong_arg_count_returns_undef, mod_i64_min_neg1_returns_undef. These are restored from main but now test mod in the two-arg section

Implement mod in the two-arg section (after pow, before trig). Use binary() helper with Int-only match: guards for y==0 and i64::MIN%-1 overflow. Remove the separate 'Integer functions' section comment

Write failing tests for clamp Real: clamp_real_within_range, clamp_real_below_lo, clamp_real_above_hi, clamp_at_lo_boundary, clamp_at_hi_boundary, clamp_nan_x_returns_undef (NEW: x.is_nan() guard), clamp_nan_lo_returns_undef, clamp_nan_hi_returns_undef, clamp_inverted_range_real_returns_undef. Use assert_real_approx! macro where applicable
Implement clamp Real arm in eval_builtin using ternary(). Guard with x.is_nan() (NEW) and valid_f64_range(lo, hi). Apply sanitize_value to result
Write failing tests for clamp Int: clamp_int_preserves_type (within range), clamp_int_below_lo (NEW), clamp_int_above_hi (NEW), clamp_inverted_range_int_returns_undef
Implement clamp Int arm: guard lo > hi returns Undef, use i64::clamp(). No NaN possible for Int

Write failing tests for clamp Scalar + fallback: clamp_scalar_preserves_dimension, clamp_dimension_mismatch_returns_undef, clamp_inverted_range_scalar_returns_undef, clamp_scalar_nan_x_returns_undef (NEW: x NaN guard in Scalar arm), clamp_wrong_arg_count_returns_undef, clamp_fallback_scalar_reconstruction (NEW: when all args have matching non-DIMENSIONLESS dimension via mixed types, fallback should return Scalar not Real)

Implement clamp Scalar arm with x.is_nan() guard (NEW) + valid_f64_range. Implement fallback arm: check dimension consistency, use as_f64(), reconstruct Scalar when dimension is non-DIMENSIONLESS (NEW: instead of always returning Real)
Write failing tests for lerp Real: lerp_midpoint, lerp_t_zero, lerp_t_one, lerp_negative_t_extrapolation, lerp_nan_t_returns_undef. Use assert_real_approx! macro

Implement lerp Real arm using ternary(). Check t dimension is DIMENSIONLESS, extract tv via as_f64(), add explicit NaN check on tv (NEW: instead of relying on sanitize_value backstop), compute via lerp_f64 helper. Apply sanitize_value

Write failing tests for lerp Scalar + dimension: lerp_scalar_preserves_dimension, lerp_dimension_mismatch_a_b_returns_undef, lerp_t_dimensioned_returns_undef, lerp_nan_a_returns_undef (NEW), lerp_nan_b_returns_undef (NEW). Use assert_scalar_approx! where applicable
Implement lerp Scalar arm: match Scalar pairs with da==db, compute via lerp_f64, preserve dimension. Add NaN guards on a/b si_values (NEW). Implement fallback with dimension check

Write failing tests for lerp Int/edge: lerp_int_inputs_coerce_to_real (NEW: documents Int->Real coercion), lerp_wrong_arg_count_returns_undef. The Int test verifies lerp(Int(0), Int(10), Real(0.5)) returns Real(5.0)
Add lerp Int fast path (NEW): when both a and b are Int, extract as f64, compute via lerp_f64, return Real. This avoids the as_f64() fallback path and documents the coercion explicitly

Write failing tests for remap Real: remap_midpoint, remap_at_from_lo, remap_at_from_hi, remap_extrapolation, remap_inverse, remap_division_by_zero_returns_undef, remap_nan_returns_undef, remap_wrong_arg_count_returns_undef. Use assert_real_approx! macro

Implement remap Real path. For non-Scalar inputs, use quinary_f64 helper (NEW: replaces inline arg-count check). Add early-exit for from_lo==from_hi (NEW: explicit Undef instead of relying on division-by-zero producing inf->sanitize_value). Formula: to_lo + (x - from_lo) * (to_hi - to_lo) / (from_hi - from_lo)

Write failing tests for remap Scalar (NEW): remap_scalar_preserves_dimension (all 5 args same LENGTH dimension, result preserves LENGTH), remap_scalar_cross_dimension (from in LENGTH, to in TIME, result is TIME), remap_scalar_dimension_mismatch_returns_undef (x dimension differs from from_lo/from_hi), remap_scalar_to_range_mismatch_returns_undef (to_lo/to_hi have different dimensions)

Implement remap dimension-aware path (NEW): before quinary_f64 fallback, check if any arg is Scalar. If so: validate from_lo/from_hi/x share a dimension, validate to_lo/to_hi share a dimension, extract si_values, compute remap formula, return Scalar with to-range dimension. On mismatch return Undef. Non-Scalar inputs fall through to quinary_f64

design_decisions.decision: Use valid_f64_range(lo, hi) -> bool helper instead of inline NaN+inverted checks
design_decisions.rationale: The pattern 'lo.is_nan() || hi.is_nan() || lo > hi' appears 4 times in clamp (Real, Scalar, fallback-Real, fallback-Scalar). Extracting to a helper reduces duplication and ensures consistency. Returns false when range is invalid (NaN or inverted)

design_decisions.decision: Add explicit x.is_nan() guard in clamp Real/Scalar/fallback arms
design_decisions.rationale: The current implementation only guards NaN in lo/hi but relies on sanitize_value backstop for NaN in x. While sanitize_value catches it, the task explicitly requests making this guard explicit for defense-in-depth clarity. f64::clamp with NaN x returns NaN, which sanitize_value catches, but an explicit early return is clearer

design_decisions.decision: Add explicit NaN check on t after as_f64() extraction in lerp
design_decisions.rationale: as_f64() on Real(NaN) returns Some(NaN). The current code passes this through to the lerp formula which produces NaN, caught by sanitize_value. Adding tv.is_nan() check immediately after extraction is more explicit and matches the pattern in clamp

design_decisions.decision: Reconstruct Scalar in clamp fallback when dimension is non-DIMENSIONLESS
design_decisions.rationale: Currently the fallback arm always returns Real, silently dropping dimension when e.g. clamp(Int(5), Scalar{3mm}, Scalar{10mm}) is called with mixed types that share a dimension. The fix returns Scalar when all dimensions match and are non-DIMENSIONLESS

design_decisions.decision: remap dimension-aware design: validate from-range and to-range dimensions independently
design_decisions.rationale: For remap(x, from_lo, from_hi, to_lo, to_hi): x/from_lo/from_hi must share a dimension (input space), to_lo/to_hi must share a dimension (output space). The two spaces need not match (e.g. remap a LENGTH to a TIME range). Result gets the to-range dimension. This is mathematically correct: remap is a linear map between two potentially different dimensional spaces

design_decisions.decision: Use quinary_f64 helper for remap f64-only path instead of inline arg extraction
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the established pattern of unary_f64/binary_f64 helpers. Handles arg count check, as_f64() extraction, and sanitize_value in one place. The dimension-aware arms are handled separately before falling through to quinary_f64

design_decisions.decision: Move mod into two-arg section rather than separate 'Integer functions' section
design_decisions.rationale: mod is a two-arg function and logically belongs with min/max/pow in the two-arg section. The separate section header was inconsistent with the organization pattern

design_decisions.decision: Add lerp Int fast path with Real return type
design_decisions.rationale: lerp(Int, Int, t) should work without falling through to the generic as_f64() path. The fast path extracts i64 values, converts to f64, computes via lerp_f64, and returns Real. This documents the Int->Real coercion explicitly

design_decisions.decision: remap early-exit for from_lo==from_hi returns Undef explicitly
design_decisions.rationale: Currently relies on division-by-zero producing inf/NaN caught by sanitize_value. An explicit check is clearer and avoids relying on floating-point edge behavior. This is analogous to clamp's inverted range guard

design_decisions.decision: Test macros assert_real_approx! and assert_scalar_approx! use 1e-12 epsilon
design_decisions.rationale: This matches the existing test convention throughout the file. The macros take (expr, expected) for Real and (expr, expected_si, expected_dim) for Scalar, reducing ~20 repetitive match blocks to one-liners

reuse.how: Reuse as-is for all NaN/inf->Undef conversion at return points of clamp/lerp/remap
Follow the same pattern for ternary (3-arg by-ref) and quinary_f64 (5-arg f64 extraction). ternary already existed in main and is restored
Used in fallback arms and quinary_f64 for extracting numeric values from any Value variant
Used in fallback arms to check dimension consistency across mixed-type arguments. Returns DIMENSIONLESS for non-Scalar
Used for lerp t-dimension check and clamp/remap fallback Scalar reconstruction decision

reuse.what: Existing test patterns (match on variant, assert with epsilon)
reuse.how: The assert_real_approx!/assert_scalar_approx! macros encapsulate the existing pattern used ~20 times in the test module

reuse.what: min/max explicit Scalar arms pattern (from prior fix)
reuse.how: Memory confirms min/max had the same dimension-dropping bug and was fixed with explicit Scalar match arms. Apply same pattern to clamp/lerp/remap
